title: Executive Order 5896—Exemption of Persons from Compulsory Retirement for Age
text:
By virtue of the authority vested in me by section 204 of Part II of the act entitled "AN ACT Making appropriations for the Legislative Branch of the Government for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1933, and for other purposes," approved June 30, 1932, the following-named persons who have reached the retirement age prescribed for automatic separation from the service are hereby exempted from the provisions of that section:
Name
Department or Establishment
Millard J. Moore
Department of Commerce
Frank C. Skinner
"          "         "
Joseph A. Hill
"          "         "
Jasper S. Bilby
"          "         "
Timothy W. Stanton
Department of the Interior
Joseph Michaelson
Navy Department
William McLean
"           "
Edgar Wommack
"           "
William A. Newcome
Department of State
Sydney Y. Smith
"          "         "
George O. Von Nerta
Treasury Department
Joseph D. Nevius
"           "
George W. Ashworth
"           "
William D. Heins
"           "
Thomas H. Carroll
"           "
Henry B. Shuman
"           "
William B. Hamilton
"           "
George W. Koonce
War Department
Francis A. Sebring
District of Columbia
Frank H. Cole
Smithsonian Institution
Coates W. Shoemaker
"           "
William E. Rollins
Senate Office Building
The foregoing persons were temporarily exempted from the provisions of section 204 of the act above mentioned by Executive Orders Nos. 5873 and 5875 of June 30, 1932.
HERBERT HOOVER
The White House,
July 29, 1932.
